overview.title: UHNW PORTFOLIO TRANSITION ANALYSIS
overview.subtitle: $25M Tech Founder Case Study | Concentrated Equity to Institutional Diversification | February 2026

client_profile.segment: Ultra High Net Worth (UHNW) | $10M+ Investable Assets
client_profile.primary_challenge: 90% portfolio concentration in single tech sector; $18.5M unrealized gains
client_profile.primary_objective: De-risk position, generate income, fund $10M Private Foundation

recommendation_summary.action: Discovery and planning: risk tolerance assessment, cost basis review, CPA consultation, investment policy statement, and transition scenario modeling.

Systematic liquidation across 5 tranches (Months 4-8, $4.5M each; total $22.5M). Concurrent redeployment into optimized model: 55% public equity, 23% fixed income, 20% alternatives (PE + Real Estate), 2% cash. Tax liability: est. $6.86M gross / $6.01M net.

Establish $5M SBL credit facility against diversified portfolio. Seed Private Foundation without triggering additional capital gains. Ongoing rebalancing, tax-loss harvesting ($850K target), and quarterly performance reviews.
$850K tax-loss harvesting offset identified. DAF and Opportunity Zone structures available to further reduce net liability.

9.8% base-case expected return projects portfolio to approximately $85M at Year 20. Foundation reaches $10.0M target at Year 20: $5M SBL seed compounding at 2.5% net plus $70,760 annual contribution from diversified portfolio income.

tax_analysis.title: TAX TRANSITION ANALYSIS
tax_analysis.subtitle: Capital Gains Management & Tax-Efficient Liquidation Strategy | February 2026

items.notes: Top federal rate on long-term gains

phased_liquidation_schedule.label: Phased Liquidation Schedule — 5 Tranches Over 8 Months

rows.description: Identify offsetting losses in fixed income and other equity positions. Reduces net taxable gain dollar-for-dollar.
Contribute appreciated shares directly to DAF. Avoid capital gains entirely; receive fair-market-value charitable deduction.
Invest deferred gains into QOZ fund. Defer recognition and potentially exclude future appreciation after 10-year hold.
5 monthly tranches across Months 4-8 within a single tax year. To split recognition across two tax years, time the final tranche(s) after December 31.
Transfer appreciated stock into CRT. Avoid immediate gain, receive income stream, estate planning benefit.

tax_analysis.disclaimer: All tax figures are estimates. Actual liability depends on cost basis, holding period, filing status, and applicable deductions.

sbl_credit.subtitle: $5M Non-Purpose Credit Facility | Private Foundation Funding Strategy | February 2026

items.notes: Proceeds may not purchase or carry securities (Reg U)
No mandatory principal amortization; annual rate resets
Annually renewable at bank's discretion; no prepayment penalty
Fund $10M philanthropic vehicle without triggering capital gains

sbl_vs_liquidation.label: SBL vs. Outright Liquidation — Tax Arbitrage Analysis

rows.advantage: After-tax interest replaces upfront tax burden
SBL delivers full loan amount; liquidation loses tax portion

rows.factor: Est. Portfolio Return on Preserved Capital (Yr 1)

rows.advantage: Portfolio return on preserved capital minus carry cost
Tax saved + compounding opportunity cost over 10 years

rows.notes: 100% via SBL — zero additional liquidation. $70,760/yr contribution begins Year 1.
7.5% return net of 5% grantmaking + $70,760/yr client contribution
Projected $7.2M at Year 10 — seed + annual contributions
Loan retired from portfolio income — no asset sales
$10M target reached: $5M seed + $70,760/yr from portfolio income

foundation_funding_analysis.formula_note: Foundation value = Seed × (1 + Net Return)^Year + Annual Contribution × ((1 + Net Return)^Year − 1) / Net Return. Net Return = Foundation Annual Return − Annual Payout. Annual Contribution sourced from diversified portfolio income ($625K/yr).
